articles.title: Kolkata waterlogged after heavy rain, traffic disrupted across city: IMD issues thunderstorm alert for Delh... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Kolkata waterlogged after heavy rain, traffic disrupted across city:IMD issues thunderstorm alert for Delhi-NCR; tourist swept away in Goa sea
Bhopal/Jaipur/Lucknow/Patna1 day ago
Heavy rain lashed Kolkata, West Bengal, on Thursday afternoon. Nearly two hours of intense rainfall submerged several major roads across the city. Waterlogging in many areas of Kolkata and Salt Lake led to long traffic jams.
The rescue operation at the warehouse that collapsed in Taratala was also affected by the heavy rain. Ten people were killed in the accident on Wednesday. So far, 30 people have been pulled out of the debris, while several others are still feared trapped beneath the rubble.
Meanwhile, pre-monsoon showers continue across six states — Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Punjab, Himachal Pradesh, Haryana and Delhi. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a thunderstorm and rain alert for Delhi-NCR over the next few hours. Wind speeds may reach up to 70 kmph, raising the risk of falling trees and flying tent sheds.
In Goa, a tourist died after being swept away by the sea at Baga Beach on Wednesday. Sahas Ashfaq Masali (33), a resident of Karnataka, was sitting on rocks along the shoreline when strong waves struck and the rough sea swept him away.
Humidity will remain in July-August, with heavy rain in some places
According to Dr Pankaj Kumar, a member of the World Meteorological Organization, in a Super El Niño, the Pacific Ocean water becomes significantly warmer than normal. This can affect the speed, direction, and continuity of the monsoon.
The result is that the rainfall pattern becomes unbalanced. This is why this time, rainfall in Madhya Pradesh is estimated to be 90% to 92% of normal. This means that some parts of the state may experience heavy rainfall, while other regions might see long breaks.
Weather experts believe that even in the rainiest months like July and August, people might have to face heat and humidity. Therefore, this time the focus will not only be on the monsoon's entry but also on how active it remains in the coming weeks.
5 pictures of rain from across the country
Tracks at Dadar Railway Station in Mumbai were submerged in water, causing some trains to run late.
Debris came onto the roads in several districts of Arunachal Pradesh due to floods and landslides.
A young man working in a field died due to lightning strike in Begusarai, Bihar.
Trees fell on roads due to a strong storm in Pinjore, Haryana, causing traffic jams.
Heatwave in 5 states, mercury crosses 40 °C
On Wednesday, the mercury crossed 40 °C in several cities of Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Rajasthan, Haryana, and Bihar. The highest temperature in the country was recorded at 44.2 °C in Banda, Uttar Pradesh.
Meanwhile, Prayagraj in UP recorded 43.4 °C, Sri Ganganagar in Rajasthan recorded 42.5 °C, Khajuraho in MP recorded 41.2 °C, and Patna in Bihar recorded 40.6 °C. In view of the heat in Muzaffarpur, Bihar, schools up to class 8 have been closed till June 27.
Next 2 Days Weather Forecast
June 26:
- Assam, Meghalaya, and Arunachal Pradesh have an Orange Alert for heavy rain for the second consecutive day.
- Due to the monsoon, heavy rain will occur in Sikkim, Konkan-Goa, Telangana, and the coastal areas of Karnataka.
- Andhra Pradesh, Marathwada, Jharkhand, and Odisha also have an Orange Alert for heavy rain.
- A heatwave warning has been issued for Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and Vidarbha for the third consecutive day.
June 27:
- Heavy rain will occur in parts of Assam, Meghalaya, West Bengal, and Sikkim.
- Orange alert in Sikkim, Konkan-Goa, Telangana, and the coastal areas of Karnataka.
- Heavy rain warning also issued for Andhra Pradesh, Jharkhand, and Odisha.
- Heatwave alert continues for the fourth consecutive day in Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and Maharashtra (Nagpur-Amravati region).
Weather conditions in the states
Madhya Pradesh: Monsoon enters Madhya Pradesh 9 days behind schedule
The southwest monsoon has entered 15 districts in the south-eastern part of Madhya Pradesh, with the rest of the state expected to come under its influence within the next two to three days. Read More
Uttar Pradesh: Heatwave in 8 UP districts, rain alert in 26
Uttar Pradesh continues to witness fluctuating weather conditions. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave warning for eight districts on Thursday, while 26 districts are under a rain alert. Read More
Rajasthan: Storm, rain alert for 25 Rajasthan districts today
An alert for thunderstorms and rain has been issued for 25 districts of Rajasthan on Thursday as monsoon activity picks up across the state. Several areas received good pre-monsoon showers on Wednesday, while western Rajasthan continued to experience high temperatures. Read More
Bihar: Heatwave in 13 districts, rain alert in 13; storms with speeds up to 60kmph also possible
Today, there is an alert for heavy rain in 13 districts of Bihar, including Tirhut, Darbhanga, Kosi, Purnia, and Saran blocks. Pre-monsoon rains are currently occurring in the state. Along with this, there is also an effect of intense heat. The Meteorological Department has issued a heatwave alert for 13 districts, including Patna and Darbhanga.
Himachal Pradesh: Monsoon likely to be delayed, heavy rain alert on June 29-30; 29% less rain so far in June
Himachal Pradesh has received 29% less rainfall than normal so far in the month of June. According to the Meteorological Department, the state normally receives 71mm of rainfall between June 1 and 24, but this time, only 50.1mm of rainfall has occurred so far. This time, there are also chances of a delay in the monsoon.
Punjab: 2-day rain-storm alert in Punjab and Chandigarh: Monsoon likely by end of June
Punjab and Chandigarh have a yellow alert for thunderstorms and rain today and tomorrow. According to the Meteorological Department, the monsoon may arrive in the state by the end of June. Meanwhile, the state's temperature has increased by 0.7 °C. It has now reached near normal levels. The maximum temperature was 41.6 °C.
